formal_strategy_baseline: ONE_SHOT_DONCHIAN_ATR_BREAKOUT 1.0.0 on BTCUSDT-PERP; related trend background only
question: After the 5%-per-asset risk budget failed only its development drawdown gate, does a fixed conservative 3%-per-asset budget preserve the same core TSMOM profitability in still-unfetched evaluation and confirmation periods while meeting personal-account risk limits?
relationship_to_parent: Post-development follow-up to research/core-perp-volscaled-tsmom. The parent development data is calibration/exposed evidence, not an independent holdout. Only evaluation and confirmation can support this candidate.
economic_rationale: Three independent 3% asset risk budgets imply about 5.2% ex-ante portfolio risk under zero correlation before caps; chosen as a conservative personal-account budget, not by solving for the parent's exact -21.80% drawdown.
gates: Identical to parent: development maxDD >-20%; evaluation maxDD >-18%; base/stress positive, each year positive, benchmark-relative drawdown, adverse >-15%, 2/3 windows and turnover. Confirmation additionally requires each 2025/2026 subperiod positive and combined positive.
release_rule: Reuse exposed development only to check the conservative budget; fetch 2022-03..2025-01 evaluation only if it passes; fetch 2024-03..2026-06 confirmation only if evaluation passes.
study_code_revision_reason: After calibration PnL but before any holdout release, fix wrapper gate key from nonexistent worst_daily_intraday... to emitted worst_daily_intrabar...; economic results, rules and thresholds unchanged.
